title: [YouTube] 5 Samvayas – Part 1 (Dr. Sanjiv Godha)
description: An accessible and profound introduction to the '5 Samvayas' (the five co-existing factors of causality in Jain philosophy) presented by Dr. Sanjiv Godha. This video dismantles the misconception that outcomes are driven by human effort alone. By breaking down how Purushartha (effort), Kaal (time), Bhavitavyata (destiny), Niyati (nature), and Karma/Nimitta work in unison, Dr. Godha offers a grounded perspective on why every event requires a harmony of multiple cooperating causes.

title: [YouTube] 5 Samvayas – Part 2 (Dr. Sanjiv Godha)
description: In this follow-up session, Dr. Sanjiv Godha dives deeper into the practical and psychological applications of the 5 Samvayas. This episode shifts from theory to real-world utility, exploring how understanding these five causal factors can dramatically alter how we process personal success, handle failure, and channel human effort. It serves as a spiritual antidote to anxiety and ego, teaching viewers how to act with dedication while remaining detached from the ultimate results.

title: [YouTube] Nimitta – Upadana Playlist (Swanubhuti Jain)
description: A comprehensive, multi-part lecture series by Swanubhuti Jain dedicated entirely to the core Jain concept of Nimitta (instrumental/external cause) and Upadana (substantial/internal cause). Through clear analogies and step-by-step reasoning, this playlist illuminates a profound truth: while external factors (Nimitta) can act as catalysts or assistants, true transformation and change always originate from within the substance itself (Upadana). Ideal for viewers seeking a systematic, detailed study of self-reliance and spiritual metaphysics.

title: [YouTube] 5 Samvayas Explained (Dharm Aur Adhyatm)
description: A highly engaging and concise explanation of the five (and often interconnected six) Samvayas, delivered in lucid Hindi. Created by the 'Dharm Aur Adhyatm' channel, this video excels at bridging ancient Jain metaphysics with modern life. Packed with relatable, everyday examples, it helps viewers understand the hidden mechanics behind daily occurrences, making it an excellent starting point for anyone looking to improve their decision-making and inner peace through spiritual philosophy.
